perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked orally by any party hereto, and such revocation shall be effective immediately for all parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the revocation terms less stringent, potentially conflicting with regulations requiring written notice for significant changes in contractual agreements related to securities filings.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Section 29(a) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 29(a) states that any condition, stipulation, or provision binding any person to waive compliance with any provision of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder, shall be void. Allowing oral revocation with immediate effect could be interpreted as a waiver of the right to proper and formal notification, which is important for compliance with SEC regulations, therefore conflicting with this section.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 78cc - Validity of contracts | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78cc - Validity of contracts U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Waiver provisions Any condition, stipulation, or provision binding any person to waive compliance with any provision of this chapter or of any rule or regulation thereunder, or of any rule of a self-regulatory organization , shall be void. (b) Contract provisions in violation of chapter Every contract made in violation of any provision of this chapter or of any rule or regulation thereunder, and every contract (including any contract for listing a security on an exchange ) heretofore or hereafter made, the performance of which involves the violation of, or the continuance of any relationship or practice in violation of, any provision of this chapter or any rule or regulation thereunder, shall be void (1) as regards the rights of any person who, in violation of any such provision, rule, or regulation, shall have made or engaged in the performance of any such contract, and (2) as regards the rights of any person who, not being a party to such contract, shall have acquired any right thereunder with actual knowledge of the facts by reason of which the making or performance of such contract was in violation of any such provision, rule, or regulation: Provided, (A) That no contract shall be void by reason of this subsection because of any violation of any rule or regulation prescribed pursuant to paragraph (3) of subsection (c) of section 78o of this title , and (B) that no contract shall be deemed to be void by reason of this subsection in any action maintained in reliance upon this subsection, by any person to or for whom any broker or dealer sells, or from or for whom any broker or dealer purchases, a security in violation of any rule or regulation prescribed pursuant to paragraph (1) or (
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for oral revocation of the agreement, effective immediately for all parties, while the original text required a signed writing. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation highlight that this change could be interpreted as a waiver of the right to proper and formal notification, which is important for compliance with SEC regulations. Scraped Law Snippet 1 and 2, specifically 15 USC 78cc (a), state that "Any condition, stipulation, or provision binding any person to waive compliance with any provision of this chapter or of any rule or regulation thereunder...shall be void.". By allowing oral revocation instead of requiring a signed writing, the changed text potentially waives compliance with regulations requiring written notification, thus contradicting the law. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - Legal Contradiction' definition, where the modification introduces a time-sensitive obligation that violates federal or state law.

perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: None of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings or any amendments thereto, nor for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning any person contained therein, regardless of whether such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the responsibility for timely and accurate filings, directly contradicting SEC regulations that mandate accuracy and timeliness in securities filings.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Section 13(d) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 13(d) requires any person who acquires beneficial ownership of more than 5 percent of a class of certain registered securities to file a statement with the SEC. This statement must include accurate information. By stating that no one is responsible for the accuracy or timeliness of filings, the modified text directly contradicts this legal obligation.

Periodical and other reports (a) Reports by issuer of security; contents Every issuer of a security registered pursuant to section 78l of this title shall file with the Commission, in accordance with such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate for the proper protection of investors and to insure fair dealing in the security- (1) such information and documents (and suc
perturbation.justification: The modified text states that no one is responsible for the accuracy or timeliness of filings. This directly contradicts the legal obligation for issuers of securities to file accurate and timely reports with the SEC, as evidenced by 15 USC 78m, which mandates issuers to file information and documents with the Commission according to its rules and regulations to protect investors and ensure fair dealing. The modified text eliminates this responsibility, creating a clear contradiction. Scraped Law Snippet 2 also references 15 USC 78m, reinforcing the legal obligation for periodical and other reports.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement must be executed in a single original document. Copies or counterparts are not permitted.
perturbation.explanation: This alteration restricts the accepted method of signing agreements which can contradict the Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (E-SIGN Act).
perturbation.contradicted_law: Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (E-SIGN Act)
perturbation.law_explanation: The E-SIGN Act establishes that electronic signatures and records have the same legal validity as traditional paper documents and signatures. By requiring a single original document and disallowing copies/counterparts, the modified text undermines the legal validity of electronic signatures, conflicting with the E-SIGN Act.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for agreements to be executed in counterparts, whereas the changed text mandates a single original document, prohibiting copies or counterparts. The LLM-generated law explanation states that the E-SIGN Act gives electronic signatures the same legal validity as traditional signatures and documents. By disallowing copies/counterparts, the modified text undermines the validity of electronic signatures, thereby contradicting the E-SIGN Act.
